text: Design is the fundamental soul of a human-made creation that ends up expressing itself in successive outer layers of the product or service.
When something is well made, you can feel the care that has gone into it.
Simplicity is not the absence of clutter, that's a consequence of simplicity. Simplicity is somehow essentially describing the purpose and place of an object and product.
I think if you do something and it turns out pretty good, then you should go do something else wonderful, not dwell on it for too long. Just figure out what's next.
A designer is an emerging synthesis of artist, inventor, mechanic, objective economist and evolutionary strategist.
Less, but better.
Good design is as little design as possible.
Good design is innovative.
Good design makes a product useful.
Good design is honest.
Good design is long-lasting. It avoids being fashionable and therefore never appears antiquated.
Indifference towards people and the reality in which they live can actually be the one and only cardinal sin in design.
Attractive things work better.
User-centered design means working with your users.
Complexity is good. Complicated is bad.
A brilliant solution to the wrong problem can be worse than no solution at all: solve the correct problem.
If you can design one thing, you can design everything.
Design is one — it is not many. The discipline of design is one.
I like design to be visually powerful, intellectually elegant, and above all timeless.
It took me a few seconds to draw it, but it took me 34 years to learn how to draw it in a few seconds.
You can't really be a designer unless you're an optimist, because you have to believe that things can be better.
A designer is a planner with an aesthetic sense — but the design depends largely on constraints.
Recognize the need. Recognize the constraints. Recognize the means.
The details are not the details. They make the design.
Design is the method of putting form and content together. Design, just as art, has multiple definitions; there is no single definition.
Simplicity is not a goal. It is a by-product of a good idea and modest expectations.
Intuition is the aggregate of all the knowledge you have acquired during your life.
Design is thinking made visual.
Form follows function — that has been misunderstood. Form and function should be one, joined in a spiritual union.
I think that the computer is an incredible tool. But you have to be very careful about what it's doing to your thinking.
Design is one of the few professions that is fundamentally optimistic — we believe things can be better.
Design is not just about the object you create; it's the system around it.
Most advanced, yet acceptable.
What is design? It's where you stand with a foot in two worlds — the world of technology and the world of people and human purposes.
Usability is not a quality that can be spread on top of a system at the end of the design process. It has to be built in from the start.
Design is the conscious effort to impose meaning.
If you're not failing, you're not pushing the boundaries of what's possible.
Simplicity is about subtracting the obvious and adding the meaningful.
source: John Maeda, The Laws of Simplicity

text: Computational design is design that would not be possible without computation.
The best work happens at the intersection of design, technology, and business.
AI is not replacing designers — it is becoming a co-author.
Before asking if AI can do it, ask if it needs to be done at all.
